typecheck_backend.details: Success: no issues found in 39 source files (after fixing 1 residual error in seed_service.py:24)

highlights: EC2 IAM role has overly broad AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess — should scope to ArtifactBucket ARN
S3 bucket publicly accessible for videos/screenshots — consider CloudFront with signed URLs
RDS DeletionProtection=false — should default true for production
EC2 placed in public subnet — best practice is private subnet behind ALB
curl|bash pattern for NodeSource — supply-chain risk
rm -rf safety: ${WWW_DIR} should use ${WWW_DIR:?} to fail on empty
Extra env vars (SECRET_KEY, LOG_LEVEL, RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE) not in Settings class
ReadWritePaths scope may need expansion for Playwright temp files
Duplicate apply_suppression call in audit_orchestrator.py (dead code)
Unnecessary 'import time as _time' inside while loop in benchmark_orchestrator.py
EVIDENCE_TYPE_LABELS type weakened from dict[EvidenceType, str] to dict[str, str]
Inconsistent session_factory typing (Any vs Callable[[], Session])
Dead 'result' variable on early return path in seed_service.py
Overly verbose return pattern in benchmark_orchestrator.py

validatorFixes.description: Fixed residual mypy error: changed 'return existing_id' to 'return str(existing_id)' to resolve 'Returning Any from function declared to return str | None' error missed by deploy-mypy-fixes worker

appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/deployment-architecture.md documenting production deployment conventions: target OS (Amazon Linux 2023), directory layout, service user (ec2-user), service ports, and key infrastructure files

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify mypy strictness level. Currently says 'mypy strict with --ignore-missing-imports' but the actual command in services.yaml does not include --strict. Align documentation with the actual enforcement level.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: deploy-mypy-fixes achieved mypy exit 0 without --strict. services.yaml command is 'mypy app/ --ignore-missing-imports' (no --strict).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: when resolving mypy type errors, prefer cast() for narrowing return types over widening container types. Use 'type: ignore[specific-code]' only for third-party library issues. Avoid intermediate-variable patterns when a simpler fix exists.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: deploy-mypy-fixes used inconsistent approaches across files: cast() in taxonomy/rule_engine/suppression, type widening for EVIDENCE_TYPE_LABELS, and intermediate typed variables in seed_service/benchmark_orchestrator.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: production deployment scripts should install requirements.txt only (not requirements-dev.txt). Dev dependencies (pytest, ruff, mypy) should not be present in production environments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: deploy-scripts-and-configs installs requirements-dev.txt in production deploy.sh (line 112).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The backend-dev skill's TDD mandate ('Write Tests First') should note an exception for pure code-quality tasks (mypy fixes, lint fixes, infrastructure file creation) where the tests validate static properties rather than runtime behavior. Implementation-first is more efficient for these tasks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both deploy-scripts-and-configs and deploy-mypy-fixes workers deviated from TDD. deploy-scripts-and-configs wrote all 4 infra files first, then tests. deploy-mypy-fixes wrote no new tests at all (correct for type annotations). Both are pragmatically justified deviations.

rejectedObservations.observation: commitId references HEAD at handoff time, not a dedicated feature commit
rejectedObservations.reason: Workflow artifact of how orchestrator assigns commitIds — not actionable for shared state

rejectedObservations.observation: First worker session (53847cb3) has no handoff file
rejectedObservations.reason: Operational artifact of a failed/partial session — not a knowledge gap for future workers
cfn-lint is not installed in the environment. Adding a broken command would be misleading. The existing Python tests provide adequate structural validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: 4-session convergence lessons for large type-fix tasks
rejectedObservations.reason: Orchestrator-level lesson about task decomposition — outside scope of shared state. Feature description was already updated with detailed instructions between attempts.
